title: CLAUDE.md
text: fastRhockey R package accessing hockey data NHL PWHL via public web APIs. package also contains deprecated functions defunct PHF league. Part SportsDataverse family. R >= 4.0.0 | License: MIT | testthat edition 3 | Version: 1.0.0

title: CLAUDE.md
code: R/                          # Source code (one function per file)   utils.R                   # Shared helpers: check_status, most_recent_*_season, messaging   zzz.R                     # .onLoad() — xG model download/caching lifecycle   fastRhockey-package.R     # Package-level roxygen2 documentation, lifecycle import   helpers_nhl.R             # helper_nhl_calculate_xg(), helper_nhl_prepare_xg_data()   helpers_nhl_edge.R        # .nhl_edge_api(), .nhl_edge_to_df() — NHL Edge URL builder   helpers_nhl_records.R     # .nhl_records_api() — records.nhl.com URL builder   pwhl_helpers.R            # .pwhl_api(), .pwhl_modulekit_url(), .pwhl_gc_url() — PWHL JSONP helpers   nhl_*.R                   # NHL functions (api-web.nhle.com + api.nhle.com/stats)   nhl_edge_*.R              # NHL Edge analytics (api-web.nhle.com/v1/edge/...)   nhl_cat_edge_*.R          # NHL Edge "CAT" variants (api-web.nhle.com/v1/cat/edge/...)   nhl_records_*.R           # NHL records API (records.nhl.com/site/api/...)   phf_*.R                   # PHF functions (deprecated — league defunct)   pwhl_*.R                  # PWHL functions (lscluster.hockeytech.com)  tests/testthat/             # Unit tests (testthat edition 3) man/                        # Auto-generated roxygen2 documentation data/                       # Included datasets (.rda) data-raw/                   # Scripts for generating package data (*.md files untracked)                             # Includes nhl_missing_endpoint_function_mapping.md                             # and OpenAPI 3.0.3 specs (nhl_*_openapi.{json,yaml})                             # for api-web, stats-rest, and records backends. vignettes/                  # Vignettes for pkgdown site

title: CLAUDE.md
text: NHL Web API returns clean JSON parsed jsonlite::fromJSON() NHL Edge API uses internal .nhl_edge_api(base, season, game_type, prefix) helper handles /now vs /{season}/{gameType} URL split, .nhl_edge_to_df() normalize assorted response shapes NHL Stats API uses Cayenne filter expressions query params. Note: leaders/{skaters,goalies}/{attribute} endpoint accept start/limit query parameters (returns 500); valid goalie attributes restricted savePctg, gaa, shutouts NHL Records API uses internal .nhl_records_api(resource, cayenne_exp, sort, limit, start, query) helper. records API support path-suffix filtering (franchise/{id} returns 404); use cayenneExp filtering instead HockeyTech returns JSONP Angular callbacks must regex-stripped parsing PWHL internal helpers pwhl_helpers.R: .pwhl_api() strips JSONP, .pwhl_modulekit_url() / .pwhl_gc_url() build URLs

title: CLAUDE.md
text: load_pwhl_*() helpers share .pwhl_release_loader() (pwhl_loaders.R), validates seasons, builds release URLs (release_tag, file_prefix) pair, downloads parallel optional progressr, optionally writes DBIConnection, tags output fastRhockey_data S3 class. Adding new dataset = one row catalog table top pwhl_loaders.R plus thin exported wrapper.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Edge family pairs /now /{season}/{gameType} URLs behind single wrapper takes optional season argument (default NULL → current season). Records family parallels Stats REST shape: 442 documented endpoints share https://records.nhl.com/site/api/{resource} optional cayenneExp filtering limit/start pagination, response shape {data: [...], total: N} tabular endpoints. Endpoint catalog OpenAPI 3.0.3 specs three NHL backends data-raw/nhl_*_openapi.{json,yaml}. function--endpoint mapping data-raw/nhl_missing_endpoint_function_mapping.md. regenerated data-raw/_gen_openapi.py upstream dfleis/nhl-api-docs catalog.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: load_nhl_*() helpers share .nhl_release_loader() (R/nhl_loaders.R), validates seasons (min 2011), builds release URLs (release_tag, file_prefix), downloads parallel optional progressr, optionally writes DBIConnection, tags output fastRhockey_data S3 class. DB helpers (update_nhl_db(), build_nhl_db(), get_missing_nhl_games()) live file. nhl_schedule() exposes opt-include_data_flags = FALSE parameter , set, left-joins cached nhl_games_in_data_repo index data repo adds one logical column per pre-compiled dataset (PBP, team_box, player_box, skater_box, goalie_box, game_info, game_rosters, scoring, penalties, scratches, linescore, three_stars, shifts, officials, shots_by_period, shootout).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: data-returning functions wrap output fastRhockey_data via make_fastRhockey_data(). Tests check: expect_s3_class(x, "fastRhockey_data").

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Three XGBoost models (5v5, special teams, penalty shots) downloaded .onLoad() sportsdataverse/fastRhockey-nhl-data/main/models/, cached tools::R_user_dir("fastRhockey", "cache"). Requires xgboost suggested package. Models stored .xg_env (package-level environment zzz.R) accessed via .xg_env$xg_model_5v5 etc. helpers_nhl.R.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: package uses lifecycle formal deprecation PHF functions: - PHF functions (21 functions): Deprecated v1.0.0 — league ceased operations - deprecated functions emit lifecycle::deprecate_stop() raise errors - Tests deprecated functions check lifecycle_error_deprecated class

title: CLAUDE.md
text: One exported function per file; filename matches function name httr::RETRY("GET", ...) HTTP requests janitor::clean_names() API responses tryCatch message() errors — functions return NULL failure, errors glue::glue() URL string interpolation Internal helpers prefixed . (e.g., .parse_game_rosters(), .pwhl_api()) globalVariables() utils.R suppress R CMD check NSE notes Types: feat, fix, docs, style, refactor, test, build, ci, chore Scopes: nhl, nhl-edge, nhl-records, nhl-stats, phf, pwhl, xg, pkgdown, ci, loader

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Exported: league_entity_action() (e.g., nhl_teams_roster(), pwhl_schedule()) Internal: .snake_case() leading dot (e.g., .parse_game_rosters(), .pwhl_api()) Tests: test-function_name.R Parameters: snake_case (e.g., game_id, team_abbr, season) Datasets: snake_case .rda files

title: CLAUDE.md
text: NHL Web API / Stats API: "20242025" (concatenated years) most_recent_nhl_season() returns end year numeric (e.g., 2025) most_recent_nhl_season_api_param() returns "20242025" format PWHL: numeric year (e.g., 2025) most_recent_pwhl_season() returns concluding year numeric (e.g., 2025) pwhl_season_id() maps (season, game_type) → HockeyTech season_id .pwhl_resolve_season_id(season, game_type) internal convenience wrapper

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Framework: testthat edition 3 Run : devtools::test() Environment: Set NOT_CRAN=true run full test suite locally Skip helpers: tests/testthat/helper-skip.R provides skip_nhl_test(), skip_phf_test(), skip_pwhl_test() controlled env vars RUN_NHL_TESTS, RUN_PHF_TESTS, RUN_PWHL_TESTS (NHL/PWHL default true, PHF false). CI workflow uses variable names. Pattern: skip_on_cran() + skip_*_test(), check data.frame + fastRhockey_data classes, validate nrow(x) > 0, check expected column names xG tests: additionally require skip_if_not_installed("xgboost") Deprecated functions: wrap calls suppressWarnings() tests Reproducibility: use specific game IDs/dates (e.g., game_id = 2023020001, PWHL game_id = 27)

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Never include AI tools (Claude, Copilot, ChatGPT, etc.) co-author author commit messages. Commits attributed solely human contributors made directed changes.

title: Contributing to fastRhockey
text: Use %>% (magrittr pipe) chaining Use janitor::clean_names() API responses Use httr::RETRY("GET", ...) HTTP requests (built-retry) Use glue::glue() URL string interpolation Maximum line length: strict limit, keep readable

title: Contributing to fastRhockey
text: API-calling functions must follow pattern: Functions return NULL failure – never throw errors user.
code: tryCatch(     expr = {         res <- httr::RETRY("GET", url)         check_status(res)         # ... parse and return     },     error = function(e) {         message(glue::glue("{Sys.time()}: Error in func_name: {e$message}"))         return(NULL)     } )

code: test_that("LEAGUE - Descriptive test name", {     skip_on_cran()     x <- function_name(param = value)      expect_s3_class(x, "data.frame")     expect_s3_class(x, "fastRhockey_data")     expect_true(nrow(x) > 0)      expected_cols <- c("col1", "col2")     for (col in expected_cols) {         expect_true(col %in% names(x), info = paste("Missing column:", col))     } })

text: Tests package make live API calls skipped CRAN via skip_on_cran(). run full test suite locally: NOT_CRAN=true: Set environment variable skip_on_cran() skip tests. can set .Renviron file inline: League-specific test toggles (via tests/testthat/helper-skip.R): Set running tests: CI, set .github/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml : NHL_TESTS=1, PWHL_TESTS=1, PHF_TESTS=0. GITHUB_PAT: loader functions access data GitHub repositories sportsdataverse org. Set GitHub personal access token avoid rate limits: Reproducible test data: Use specific, known game IDs, dates, seasons tests (e.g., game_id = 2023020001, season = 2024) rather most_recent_*_season() helpers, test expectations remain stable across seasons.

title: Contributing to fastRhockey
code: #' @description #' `r lifecycle::badge("deprecated")` #' #' This function is deprecated. Use [new_function()] instead. my_old_function <- function(param) {     lifecycle::deprecate_warn(         when = "1.0.0",         what = "my_old_function()",         with = "my_new_function()"     )     # Delegate to the new function     my_new_function(param) }

title: Getting Started with fastRhockey
text: can install CRAN version fastRhockey : can install development version GitHub :
code: install.packages("fastRhockey") if (!requireNamespace('pak', quietly = TRUE)){   install.packages('pak') } pak::pak("sportsdataverse/fastRhockey")

title: Getting Started with fastRhockey
text: PWHL (Professional Women’s Hockey League) launched January 2024 six teams: Boston, Minnesota, Montreal, New York, Ottawa, Toronto. fastRhockey provides full access PWHL schedules, standings, play--play, player box scores, team rosters, stat leaders.

title: Getting Started with fastRhockey
code: library(dplyr)  pwhl_teams()

title: Getting Started with fastRhockey
text: Let’s pull 2024 PWHL season schedule: schedule returns game IDs, dates, teams, scores, venue information every game season.
code: schedule <- pwhl_schedule(season = 2024)  schedule %>%   dplyr::filter(game_status == "Final") %>%   dplyr::select(game_id, game_date, home_team, away_team,                 home_score, away_score, winner, venue) %>%   head(10)

title: Getting Started with fastRhockey
code: pwhl_standings(season = 2024) %>%   dplyr::select(team_rank, team, games_played, points,                 wins, losses, goals_for, goals_against)

title: Getting Started with fastRhockey
text: Pick game schedule pull individual player stats. pwhl_player_box() returns list two data frames: skaters goalies.
code: box <- pwhl_player_box(game_id = 27)  # Skater stats box[[1]] %>%   dplyr::select(first_name, last_name, position, team_id,                 goals, assists, points, shots, toi) %>%   dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(points)) %>%   head(10)  # Goalie stats box[[2]] %>%   dplyr::select(first_name, last_name, team_id,                 saves, goals_against, shots_against, toi)

title: Getting Started with fastRhockey
text: real power fastRhockey play--play data. pwhl_pbp() returns detailed event-level data including shot locations, faceoff results, penalties, goals assist information, plus/minus player tracking. play--play data includes 90+ columns event details, player information, coordinates (raw transformed), game context.
code: pbp <- pwhl_pbp(game_id = 27)  pbp %>%   dplyr::select(event, team_id, period_of_game, time_of_period,                 player_name_first, player_name_last,                 x_coord, y_coord) %>%   head(15)

title: Getting Started with fastRhockey
text: Pull league-wide goalie skater stats:
code: # Goalie stats pwhl_stats(position = "goalie", season = 2024) %>%   dplyr::select(player_name, team, games_played,                 goals_against_avg, save_percentage, shutouts) %>%   head(10) # Skater stats (all teams) pwhl_stats(position = "skater", season = 2024) %>%   dplyr::select(player_name, team, games_played,                 goals, assists, points) %>%   head(10)

title: Getting Started with fastRhockey
text: fastRhockey also provides extensive access NHL data via api-web.nhle.com api.nhle.com/stats APIs.

title: Getting Started with fastRhockey
code: nhl_schedule(season = "20242025") %>%   head(10)

code: feed <- nhl_game_feed(game_id = 2024020001)  dplyr::glimpse(feed)

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions
text: fastRhockey R package accessing hockey data NHL (National Hockey League) PWHL (Professional Women’s Hockey League). wraps public web APIs (NHL API, HockeyTech) provide structured tibbles play--play, schedule, standings, roster, draft, player stats, team data. package also ships integrated expected goals (xG) models trained via XGBoost cached locally. package includes deprecated functions defunct PHF (Premier Hockey Federation) league. emit lifecycle warnings removed future release. Part SportsDataverse family R/Python packages sports analytics. Version: 1.0.0 R version: >= 4.0.0 License: MIT Documentation site: https://fastRhockey.sportsdataverse.org/ GitHub: https://github.com/sportsdataverse/fastRhockey

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions
code: # Install all dependencies (dev + suggests) install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_deps(dependencies = TRUE)  # Run full R CMD check devtools::check()  # Build documentation (roxygen2) devtools::document()  # Run tests devtools::test() # or testthat::test_local()  # Run a single test file testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-nhl_standings.R")  # Build and install locally devtools::install()  # Build pkgdown site pkgdown::build_site()

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions
code: R/                          # Source code (one function per file, mostly)   utils.R                   # Shared helpers: check_status, most_recent_*_season, messaging   zzz.R                     # .onLoad() — xG model download/caching lifecycle   fastRhockey-package.R     # Package-level roxygen doc, lifecycle import   helpers_nhl.R             # helper_nhl_calculate_xg(), helper_nhl_prepare_xg_data()   helpers_nhl_edge.R        # .nhl_edge_api(), .nhl_edge_to_df() — NHL Edge URL builder   helpers_nhl_records.R     # .nhl_records_api() — records.nhl.com URL builder   helpers_phf.R             # PHF helper functions (deprecated)   data.R                    # Roxygen docs for included datasets   espn_nhl_teams.R          # ESPN team reference data    # --- NHL Functions (api-web.nhle.com + api.nhle.com/stats) ---   nhl_game_feed.R           # Game feed with PBP, rosters, game info + nhl_game_pbp()   nhl_game_boxscore.R       # Game boxscore (skater/goalie stats)   nhl_game_shifts.R         # Shift data   nhl_game_story.R          # Game story/narrative   nhl_game_content.R        # Game content (media)   nhl_gamecenter.R          # Gamecenter landing & right rail   nhl_schedule.R            # Schedule (by date or full season)   nhl_schedule_calendar.R   # Calendar-formatted schedules   nhl_standings.R           # Current/historical standings   nhl_standings_season.R    # Standings season list   nhl_teams.R               # Team info   nhl_teams_roster.R        # Team rosters   nhl_teams_stats.R         # Team per-player stats   nhl_teams_info.R          # Team info by abbreviation   nhl_team_prospects.R      # Team prospects   nhl_team_scoreboard.R     # Team-specific scoreboard   nhl_player_info.R         # Player biographical info   nhl_player_game_log.R     # Player game logs   nhl_player_stats.R        # Player season stats   nhl_player_spotlight.R    # Featured player spotlight   nhl_stats_goalies.R       # Goalie stats (api.nhle.com/stats)   nhl_stats_skaters.R       # Skater stats (api.nhle.com/stats)   nhl_stats_teams.R         # Team stats (api.nhle.com/stats)   nhl_stats_misc.R          # Miscellaneous stats + draft + stats seasons   nhl_goalie_stats_leaders.R# Goalie leader rankings   nhl_skater_stats_leaders.R# Skater leader rankings   nhl_draft.R               # Draft data   nhl_draft_year.R          # Draft by year   nhl_draft_prospects.R     # Draft prospects   nhl_draft_prospects_info.R# Draft prospect details   nhl_seasons.R             # Available seasons + draft rankings   nhl_scoreboard.R          # Daily scoreboard   nhl_scores.R              # Scores   nhl_playoffs.R            # Playoff brackets, schedules, carousels   nhl_tv_schedule.R         # TV broadcast schedule   nhl_where_to_watch.R      # Where-to-watch info   nhl_meta.R                # Metadata + location + playoff-series metadata endpoints   nhl_club_schedule.R       # Club-specific schedule   nhl_club_stats_season.R   # Club-specific stats   nhl_roster_season.R       # Roster by season   nhl_conferences.R         # Conferences (derived from standings)   nhl_conferences_info.R    # Conference details   nhl_divisions.R           # Divisions (derived from standings)   nhl_divisions_info.R      # Division details   nhl_wsc_pbp.R             # WSC narrative-format play-by-play   nhl_draft_tracker.R       # Live draft tracker   nhl_ppt_replay.R          # Event-level video replay metadata   nhl_ppt_replay_goal.R     # Goal-specific replay metadata   nhl_postal_lookup.R       # Broadcast region lookup by postal code   nhl_smartlinks.R          # NHL.com smart-link router    # --- NHL Edge Analytics (33 functions) ---   # All wrappers share .nhl_edge_api() and .nhl_edge_to_df()   nhl_edge_skater_*.R       # Detail, landing, comparison, shot location/speed,                             # skating speed/distance, zone time, top-10 leaderboards   nhl_edge_goalie_*.R       # title: GitHub Copilot Instructions
text: Five distinct backends: NHL Web API returns clean JSON; parsed jsonlite::fromJSON() NHL Edge API uses internal .nhl_edge_api(base, season, game_type, prefix) helper handles /now vs /{season}/{gameType} URL split, plus .nhl_edge_to_df() normalize response shapes NHL Stats API uses Cayenne filter expressions query params (proprietary). Note: leaders/{skaters,goalies}/{attribute} endpoint accept start/limit query parameters (returns 500); valid goalie attributes restricted savePctg, gaa, shutouts NHL Records API uses internal .nhl_records_api(resource, cayenne_exp, sort, limit, start, query) helper. records API support path-suffix filtering (franchise/{id} returns 404); use cayenneExp filtering instead HockeyTech returns JSONP Angular callbacks must regex-stripped parsing

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions
text: data-returning functions wrap output fastRhockey_data S3 class via make_fastRhockey_data(). adds metadata attributes (source description, timestamp) provides custom print() method.

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions
text: package load (.onLoad zzz.R): Downloads XGBoost JSON models feature-name metadata sportsdataverse/fastRhockey-nhl-data/main/models/ Cached tools::R_user_dir("fastRhockey", "cache") — downloaded Three models: 5v5, special teams, penalty shots Models stored .xg_env (package environment zzz.R), accessed via .xg_env$xg_model_5v5 etc. helpers_nhl.R helper_nhl_calculate_xg() runs predictions; adds xg column shot-type events Requires xgboost suggested package; fails gracefully absent

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions
text: One exported function per file (filename matches function name) roxygen2 documentation; regenerate devtools::document() Pipe-first style: functions use %>% (magrittr) extensively janitor::clean_names() applied API responses consistent snake_case columns httr::RETRY(“GET”, …) HTTP requests (built-retry transient failures) tryCatch message() error handling — functions return NULL failure, errors glue::glue() string interpolation URL construction Internal helper functions prefixed . (e.g., .parse_game_rosters(), .build_pbp()) globalVariables() declarations utils.R suppress R CMD check NSE notes

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions
text: NHL Web API: "20242025" — concatenated year strings NHL Stats API Cayenne filters: "20242025" — format most_recent_nhl_season(): Returns end year numeric (e.g., 2025 2024-25 season) most_recent_nhl_season_api_param(): Returns "20242025" format PWHL: Numeric year (e.g., 2025) most_recent_pwhl_season(): Returns concluding year numeric (e.g., 2025) pwhl_season_id(): Maps (season, game_type) HockeyTech season_id dynamically

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions
code: tryCatch(     expr = {         res <- httr::RETRY("GET", url)         check_status(res)         # ... parse and return     },     error = function(e) {         message(glue::glue("{Sys.time()}: Error in function_name: {e$message}"))         return(NULL)     } )

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions
text: Framework: testthat edition 3 Location: tests/testthat/ Run : devtools::test() testthat::test_local() Test naming: test-<function_name>.R Environment: Set NOT_CRAN=true full test suite CI Skip helpers: tests/testthat/helper-skip.R provides skip_nhl_test(), skip_phf_test(), skip_pwhl_test() controlled env vars RUN_NHL_TESTS, RUN_PHF_TESTS, RUN_PWHL_TESTS (NHL/PWHL default true, PHF default false)

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions
text: Key conventions: - skip_on_cran() tests hit live APIs - Test data.frame fastRhockey_data S3 classes - Validate row counts > 0 - Check expected column names - Use specific game IDs reproducible tests (e.g., 2023020001) - xG tests additionally require skip_if_not_installed("xgboost") - Deprecated function tests: wrap calls suppressWarnings()
code: test_that("NHL - Descriptive test name", {     skip_on_cran()     skip_nhl_test()     x <- nhl_function(param = value)     expect_s3_class(x, "data.frame")     expect_s3_class(x, "fastRhockey_data")     expect_true(nrow(x) > 0)     expect_true("expected_column" %in% names(x)) })

title: fastRhockey
text: fastRhockey R package accessing hockey data NHL (National Hockey League) PWHL (Professional Women’s Hockey League) via public web APIs. provides structured data frames play--play, schedule, standings, roster, draft, player stats, team data. package also includes integrated expected goals (xG) models trained via XGBoost. Part SportsDataverse family R/Python packages sports analytics.

title: fastRhockey
text: NHL Data – Game feeds, boxscores, play--play shifts, schedules, standings, rosters, draft picks, player/team stats, playoffs, broadcast information via api-web.nhle.com api.nhle.com/stats NHL Edge Analytics (33 functions) – Skater, goalie, team advanced metrics api-web.nhle.com/v1/edge/... covering shot location, shot speed, skating speed, skating distance, zone time, comparisons, landing pages, top-10 leaderboards. wrapper accepts optional season argument falls back current season via /now form NHL Records API (25 functions) – First-time integration records.nhl.com/site/api/ franchise totals, player/skater/goalie career real-time stats, draft lottery, hall fame, trophies, awards, attendance, venues, officials, combine data NHL Stats REST (19 dedicated wrappers) – nhl_stats_franchise(), nhl_stats_players(), nhl_stats_glossary(), nhl_stats_country(), nhl_stats_skater_leaders(), nhl_stats_goalie_leaders(), nhl_stats_skater_milestones(), nhl_stats_goalie_milestones(), plus original nhl_stats_skaters() / nhl_stats_goalies() / nhl_stats_teams() / nhl_stats_draft() / nhl_stats_seasons() / nhl_stats_misc() family PWHL Data – Schedules, standings, play--play, player box scores, team rosters, stat leaders, player profiles, game logs, career stats, player search, league leaders, transactions, streaks, playoff brackets, game summaries, scorebar via HockeyTech API xG Models – Expected goals predictions NHL play--play data using XGBoost models (5v5, special teams, penalty shots) Helper Aggregators – Convenience functions inspired nhl-api-py combine multiple endpoint calls single tidy frame: nhl_game_ids_by_season(), nhl_all_players_by_season(), nhl_player_career_stats(), nhl_team_summary_range(), nhl_skater_summary_range(), nhl_goalie_summary_range() Full Season Loaders – Nineteen NHL loaders covering play--play (full + lite), schedules, season + per-game rosters, player/skater/goalie/team boxscores, game info, scoring & penalty summaries, three stars, scratches, linescores, shifts, officials, shots--period, shootouts (load_nhl_pbp(), load_nhl_pbp_lite(), load_nhl_schedule(), load_nhl_rosters(), load_nhl_game_rosters(), load_nhl_team_box(), load_nhl_player_box(), load_nhl_skater_box(), load_nhl_goalie_box(), load_nhl_game_info(), load_nhl_scoring(), load_nhl_penalties(), load_nhl_three_stars(), load_nhl_scratches(), load_nhl_linescore(), load_nhl_shifts(), load_nhl_officials(), load_nhl_shots_by_period(), load_nhl_shootout()); PWHL, fifteen loaders covering play--play, schedules, season + per-game rosters, player/skater/goalie/team boxscores, game info, scoring & penalty summaries, three stars, officials, shots--period, shootouts (load_pwhl_pbp(), load_pwhl_schedule(), load_pwhl_rosters(), load_pwhl_game_rosters(), load_pwhl_player_box(), load_pwhl_skater_box(), load_pwhl_goalie_box(), load_pwhl_team_box(), load_pwhl_game_info(), load_pwhl_scoring_summary(), load_pwhl_penalty_summary(), load_pwhl_three_stars(), load_pwhl_officials(), load_pwhl_shots_by_period(), load_pwhl_shootout()) Note: PHF (Premier Hockey Federation) functions deprecated v1.0.0. league ceased operations; use PWHL functions instead.

code: install.packages("fastRhockey") # You can install using the pak package using the following code: if (!requireNamespace('pak', quietly = TRUE)){   install.packages('pak') } pak::pak("sportsdataverse/fastRhockey") if (!requireNamespace('remotes', quietly = TRUE)){   install.packages('remotes') } remotes::install_github(repo = "sportsdataverse/fastRhockey")

what: Calculate expected goals (xG) for play-by-play data — helper_nhl_calculate_xg
title: Calculate expected goals (xG) for play-by-play data — helper_nhl_calculate_xg
text: Adds xg column play--play data frame using fastRhockey xG models (XGBoost). models trained NHL PBP data 2010-2024 using methodology hockeyR xG models. Three models used: 5v5 model — Even-strength situations Special teams model — Power play, short-handed, non-5v5 situations (adds skater count features) Penalty shot model — constant xG value penalty shot attempts Models loaded package namespace package load via .onLoad() (see zzz.R).

title: Calculate expected goals (xG) for play-by-play data — helper_nhl_calculate_xg
code: helper_nhl_calculate_xg(pbp)

title: Calculate expected goals (xG) for play-by-play data — helper_nhl_calculate_xg
text: pbp play--play data frame produced nhl_game_pbp nhl_game_feed. Must contain columns needed helper_nhl_prepare_xg_data(): event_type, secondary_type, period_type, period, game_seconds, x, y, x_fixed, event_team_abbr, home_abbr, away_abbr, home_skaters, away_skaters, shot_distance, shot_angle, empty_net, strength_state, event_id, season.

title: Calculate expected goals (xG) for play-by-play data — helper_nhl_calculate_xg
text: original pbp data frame xg column appended. xg NA non-shot events.

title: Calculate expected goals (xG) for play-by-play data — helper_nhl_calculate_xg
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   pbp <- nhl_game_pbp(2024020001, include_shifts = TRUE)   pbp <- helper_nhl_calculate_xg(pbp)   # View xG for shots   pbp[!is.na(pbp$xg), c("event_type", "shot_distance", "shot_angle", "xg")] } # }

what: Prepare PBP data for xG model predictions — helper_nhl_prepare_xg_data
title: Prepare PBP data for xG model predictions — helper_nhl_prepare_xg_data
text: Helper function prepare fastRhockey PBP data expected goals (xG) calculations. Adapted hockeyR helper_nhl_prepare_xg_data() function. feature engineering logic (lag events, era dummies, shot metrics, one-hot encoding) identical hockeyR xG training pipeline xgboost models can used inference.

title: Prepare PBP data for xG model predictions — helper_nhl_prepare_xg_data
code: helper_nhl_prepare_xg_data(x)

title: Prepare PBP data for xG model predictions — helper_nhl_prepare_xg_data
text: x play--play data frame produced nhl_game_pbp nhl_game_feed.

title: Prepare PBP data for xG model predictions — helper_nhl_prepare_xg_data
text: tibble model features plus identifiers (season, game_id, event_id, strength_state).

title: Prepare PBP data for xG model predictions — helper_nhl_prepare_xg_data
text: function: Filters shootouts, penalty shots, shift-change events. Computes lag features (last event type/team, time since last, distance last, event zones). Adds era dummies four training eras (2011-13, 2014-18, 2019-21, 2022+). Computes tactical booleans: rebound, rush, cross_ice_event. One-hot encodes shot_type last_event_type. Pads missing model features zero xgboost DMatrix dimensions match trained models.

title: NHL Club Schedule — nhl_club_schedule
text: Returns schedule data given team. Supports season, month, week views.

title: NHL Club Schedule — nhl_club_schedule
code: nhl_club_schedule(   team_abbr,   season = NULL,   month = NULL,   view = "season",   date = NULL )

title: NHL Club Schedule — nhl_club_schedule
text: team_abbr Three-letter team abbreviation (e.g., "TOR", "BOS") season Integer 4-digit end year season (e.g., 2026 2025-26 season), matching most_recent_nhl_season(). NULL, returns current season schedule. month Character month "YYYY-MM" format (e.g., "2025-01"). provided, returns month's schedule. Ignored view "week". view Character: "season" (default), "month", "week". date Character date "YYYY-MM-DD" format week view. NULL view="week", returns current week.

what: NHL Edge Team Zone Time Top 10 — nhl_edge_team_zone_time_top_10
title: NHL Edge Team Zone Time Top 10 — nhl_edge_team_zone_time_top_10
text: Returns NHL Edge top-10 team zone-time leaderboard. Wraps https://api-web.nhle.com/v1/edge/team-zone-time-top-10/{strength}/{sortBy}/.... season NULL (default) /now endpoint used fetch current season.

title: NHL Edge Team Zone Time Top 10 — nhl_edge_team_zone_time_top_10
code: nhl_edge_team_zone_time_top_10(strength, sort_by, season = NULL, game_type = 2)

title: NHL Edge Team Zone Time Top 10 — nhl_edge_team_zone_time_top_10
text: strength Character strength state (e.g., "", "ev", "pp", "pk"). sort_by Character metric sort leaderboard (e.g., "offensive", "defensive", "neutral"). season Optional 4-digit end-year (e.g., 2025 2024-25 season), 8-character API season (e.g., "20242025"), NULL (default) current season via /now endpoint. game_type Integer game type. 1 = preseason, 2 = regular season (default), 3 = playoffs.

title: NHL Edge Team Zone Time Top 10 — nhl_edge_team_zone_time_top_10
text: fastRhockey_data tibble top-10 team leaderboard, NULL failure / empty response.

title: NHL Game Boxscore — nhl_game_boxscore
text: Retrieve boxscore data specific NHL game NHL web API (api-web.nhle.com).

title: NHL Game Boxscore — nhl_game_boxscore
code: nhl_game_boxscore(game_id)

title: NHL Game Boxscore — nhl_game_boxscore
text: game_id (integer) NHL game ID, e.g. 2024020001.

title: NHL Game Boxscore — nhl_game_boxscore
text: named list containing: game_info — one-row tibble game metadata (id, season, game type, date, venue, teams, final score, shots goal). team_box — two-row tibble (away / home) team-level totals. skater_stats — tibble skater (forward + defense) individual stats teams. goalie_stats — tibble goalie individual stats teams.

title: NHL Game Boxscore — nhl_game_boxscore
text: Uses endpoint https://api-web.nhle.com/v1/gamecenter/{game_id}/boxscore.

title: NHL Game Boxscore — nhl_game_boxscore
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   box <- nhl_game_boxscore(2024020001)   box$game_info   box$skater_stats } # }

title: NHL Game Content — nhl_game_content
text: Returns game summary content given game ID. Uses new NHL API gamecenter landing endpoint (api-web.nhle.com). Note: original statsapi.web.nhl.com endpoint retired. function now returns game summary data (teams, scoring, three stars) gamecenter landing page instead old highlights/content data.

title: NHL Game Content — nhl_game_content
code: nhl_game_content(game_id)

title: NHL Game Content — nhl_game_content
text: game_id Game unique ID (e.g., 2024020001)

title: NHL Game Content — nhl_game_content
text: Returns data frame game summary information including teams, scores, venue, period scoring details.

title: NHL Game Feed — nhl_game_feed
text: Returns named list play--play data plus game metadata given NHL game. Uses new NHL API api-web.nhle.com.

title: NHL Game Feed — nhl_game_feed
code: nhl_game_feed(game_id, include_shifts = TRUE, raw = FALSE)

title: NHL Game Feed — nhl_game_feed
text: game_id Game unique ID (e.g. 2024020001) include_shifts Logical; whether integrate shift data -ice player tracking. Default TRUE. raw Logical; TRUE, return unprocessed API response list instead parsed/enriched data. Default FALSE.

title: NHL Game Feed — nhl_game_feed
text: named list elements: pbp (play--play data frame), game_info (game metadata), rosters (player roster data frame). raw = TRUE, returns raw JSON response nested list.

what: NHL Game IDs by Season — nhl_game_ids_by_season
title: NHL Game IDs by Season — nhl_game_ids_by_season
text: Aggregator helper iterates every NHL team's season schedule (via nhl_club_schedule()) returns deduplicated tidy data frame game IDs requested season game types. Mirrors Helpers.game_ids_by_season convenience helper nhl-api-py Python client.

title: NHL Game IDs by Season — nhl_game_ids_by_season
code: nhl_game_ids_by_season(   season,   game_types = c(2L),   team_abbr = NULL,   sleep_rate = 0 )

title: NHL Game IDs by Season — nhl_game_ids_by_season
text: season Integer four-digit end year season (e.g. 2025 2024-25 season). Passed nhl_club_schedule() uses end-year convention. game_types Integer vector NHL game types keep. 1 = preseason, 2 = regular season (default), 3 = playoffs. team_abbr Optional character three-letter team abbreviation (e.g. "TOR"). supplied, team's schedule fetched instead iterating 32 NHL clubs. sleep_rate Numeric seconds Sys.sleep() per-team API calls iterating teams. Defaults 0 (delay).

title: NHL Game IDs by Season — nhl_game_ids_by_season
text: fastRhockey_data / data.frame one row per unique game, containing game_id, season, game_type, game_date, home_team, away_team. Returns NULL outer failure.

title: NHL Game IDs by Season — nhl_game_ids_by_season
NHL Game Play-by-Play — nhl_game_pbp
text: Returns detailed play--play data given NHL game, including event players, coordinates, -ice skaters, strength states, shot distance/angle, shift integration. Uses new NHL API api-web.nhle.com.

title: NHL Game Play-by-Play — nhl_game_pbp
code: nhl_game_pbp(game_id, include_shifts = TRUE, raw = FALSE)

title: NHL Game Play-by-Play — nhl_game_pbp
text: game_id Game unique ID (e.g. 2024020001) include_shifts Logical; whether integrate shift data -ice player tracking. Default TRUE. raw Logical; TRUE, return unprocessed API response list instead parsed data frame. Default FALSE.

title: NHL Game Play-by-Play — nhl_game_pbp
text: data frame one row per event (default), raw API response list raw = TRUE.

title: NHL Goalie Stats Leaders — nhl_goalie_stats_leaders
text: Returns league-wide goalie statistical leaders given season game type. Supports multiple stat categories.

title: NHL Goalie Stats Leaders — nhl_goalie_stats_leaders
code: nhl_goalie_stats_leaders(   season = NULL,   game_type = 2,   categories = NULL,   limit = NULL )

title: NHL Goalie Stats Leaders — nhl_goalie_stats_leaders
text: season Integer 4-digit year (e.g., 2024 2024-25 season). NULL, returns current season leaders. game_type Integer game type: 2 = regular season (default), 3 = playoffs categories Character vector stat categories (e.g., "wins", "gaa", "savePctg", "shutouts"). NULL, returns available categories. limit Integer maximum number leaders per category. NULL, uses API default.

title: NHL Goalie Stats Leaders — nhl_goalie_stats_leaders
text: Returns data frame goalie leaders.

title: NHL Goalie Summary (Season Range) — nhl_goalie_summary_range
text: Aggregator helper calls nhl_stats_goalies() report_type = "summary" every season [start_season, end_season] concatenates results single tidy frame. Mirrors Stats.goalie_stats_summary convenience helper nhl-api-py Python client.

title: NHL Goalie Summary (Season Range) — nhl_goalie_summary_range
code: nhl_goalie_summary_range(start_season, end_season, game_type = 2, limit = 50)

title: NHL Goalie Summary (Season Range) — nhl_goalie_summary_range
text: start_season Integer four-digit end year first season (e.g. 2022 2021-22 season). end_season Integer four-digit end year final season (inclusive). Must >= start_season. game_type Integer game type: 2 = regular season (default), 3 = playoffs. limit Integer maximum number rows per season request. Defaults 50 match nhl_stats_goalies().

title: NHL Goalie Summary (Season Range) — nhl_goalie_summary_range
text: fastRhockey_data / data.frame concatenated goalie summary stats added season column marking season row came . Returns NULL outer failure.

title: NHL Location — nhl_location
text: Returns country/location configuration data NHL API.

title: NHL Location — nhl_location
code: nhl_location()

title: NHL Location — nhl_location
text: Returns list location data.

title: NHL Location — nhl_location
code: # \donttest{   try(nhl_location()) #> $country #> [1] "US" #>  # }

title: NHL Meta — nhl_meta
text: Returns NHL metadata (players, teams, season states, game types). Three branches supported: year series_letter supplied — returns playoff-series metadata via meta/playoff-series/{year}/{seriesLetter}. game_id supplied — returns per-game metadata via meta/game/{game_id}. arguments — returns general league meta via meta.

title: NHL Meta — nhl_meta
code: nhl_meta(game_id = NULL, year = NULL, series_letter = NULL)

title: NHL Meta — nhl_meta
text: game_id Optional game ID. provided (year / series_letter ), returns metadata game. year Optional integer playoff year (e.g., 2024). Must supplied together series_letter fetch playoff-series metadata. series_letter Optional single-letter playoff series identifier (e.g., ""). Must supplied together year.

title: NHL Meta — nhl_meta
text: Returns list NHL metadata.

title: NHL Meta — nhl_meta

title: NHL Player Career Stats — nhl_player_career_stats
text: Aggregator helper combines biographical information NHL player landing endpoint player's season--season career totals. Returns single multi-row "career--season" data frame. Mirrors Stats.player_career_stats convenience helper nhl-api-py Python client.

title: NHL Player Career Stats — nhl_player_career_stats
code: nhl_player_career_stats(player_id)

title: NHL Player Career Stats — nhl_player_career_stats
text: player_id Integer player ID (e.g. 8478402 Connor McDavid).

title: NHL Player Career Stats — nhl_player_career_stats
text: fastRhockey_data / data.frame one row per season played. Always includes player_id, first_name, last_name, position, season, game_type_id, plus season stat columns exposed landing endpoint's seasonTotals payload (e.g. goals, assists, points, games_played, pim, shots, team_name_default, league_abbrev, ...). Returns NULL failure.

title: NHL Player Game Log — nhl_player_game_log
text: Returns game--game stats NHL player. Supports current season historical season lookups.

title: NHL Player Game Log — nhl_player_game_log
code: nhl_player_game_log(player_id, season = NULL, game_type = 2)

title: NHL Player Game Log — nhl_player_game_log
text: player_id Integer player ID (e.g., 8478402 Connor McDavid) season Integer 4-digit year (e.g., 2024 2024-25 season). NULL, returns current season game log. game_type Integer game type: 2 = regular season (default), 3 = playoffs

title: NHL Player Game Log — nhl_player_game_log
text: Returns data frame game--game statistics.

title: NHL Player Stats — nhl_player_stats
text: Returns season--season stats given player ID. Uses new NHL API player landing endpoint (api-web.nhle.com). Note: original statsapi.web.nhl.com endpoint retired. function now returns seasonTotals data player landing page, includes year--year stats across leagues (NHL, AHL, junior, etc.).

title: NHL Player Stats — nhl_player_stats
code: nhl_player_stats(player_id)

title: NHL Player Stats — nhl_player_stats
text: player_id Player unique ID (e.g., 8476899)

title: NHL Player Stats — nhl_player_stats
text: Returns data frame season--season stats player, including games played, goals, assists, league-specific columns.

title: NHL Postal Lookup — nhl_postal_lookup
text: Returns broadcast region / geo-lookup information given postal (ZIP) code NHL web service endpoint postal-lookup/{postalCode}. Used internally NHL.com determine local broadcast rights.

title: NHL Postal Lookup — nhl_postal_lookup
code: nhl_postal_lookup(postal_code)

title: NHL Postal Lookup — nhl_postal_lookup
text: postal_code Character. Postal ZIP code look (e.g., "10001" New York City).

title: NHL Postal Lookup — nhl_postal_lookup

title: NHL Schedule — nhl_schedule
text: Returns NHL schedule data given day season. Uses NHL API (api-web.nhle.com).

title: NHL Schedule — nhl_schedule
code: nhl_schedule(   day = NULL,   season = NULL,   team_abbr = NULL,   include_data_flags = FALSE )

title: NHL Schedule — nhl_schedule
text: day Character date "YYYY-MM-DD" format. provided, returns games specific day. season Integer four-digit year end year season (e.g., 2026 2025-26 season), matching most_recent_nhl_season(). provided instead day, returns full season schedule. team_abbr Character three-letter team abbreviation (e.g., "TOR"). Required season used. NULL, loops teams. include_data_flags Logical (default FALSE). TRUE, building live schedule result left-joined pre-compiled nhl_games_in_data_repo index sportsdataverse-data add per-game data-availability flags (PBP, team_box, player_box, skater_box, goalie_box, game_info, game_rosters, scoring, penalties, scratches, linescore, three_stars, shifts, officials, shots_by_period, shootout). Games yet compiled get FALSE. requires network call data repo adds small delay.

title: NHL Schedule — nhl_schedule
text: Returns data frame game schedule information. include_data_flags = TRUE additionally carries one logical column per pre-compiled dataset.

title: NHL Scores — nhl_scores
text: Returns scores games given date.

title: NHL Scores — nhl_scores
code: nhl_scores(date = NULL)

title: NHL Scores — nhl_scores
text: date Character date "YYYY-MM-DD" format. NULL, returns current scores.

title: NHL Scores — nhl_scores
text: Returns data frame game scores.

title: NHL Seasons — nhl_seasons
text: Returns list NHL seasons metadata.

title: NHL Seasons — nhl_seasons
code: nhl_seasons()

title: NHL Seasons — nhl_seasons
text: Returns data frame season information.

title: NHL Skater Stats Leaders — nhl_skater_stats_leaders
text: Returns league-wide skater statistical leaders given season game type. Supports multiple stat categories.

title: NHL Skater Stats Leaders — nhl_skater_stats_leaders
code: nhl_skater_stats_leaders(   season = NULL,   game_type = 2,   categories = NULL,   limit = NULL )

title: NHL Skater Stats Leaders — nhl_skater_stats_leaders
text: season Integer 4-digit year (e.g., 2024 2024-25 season). NULL, returns current season leaders. game_type Integer game type: 2 = regular season (default), 3 = playoffs categories Character vector stat categories (e.g., "goals", "assists", "points", "plusMinus", "penaltyMins"). NULL, returns available categories. limit Integer maximum number leaders per category. NULL, uses API default.

title: NHL Skater Stats Leaders — nhl_skater_stats_leaders
text: Returns data frame skater leaders.

title: NHL Skater Summary (Season Range) — nhl_skater_summary_range
text: Aggregator helper calls nhl_stats_skaters() report_type = "summary" every season [start_season, end_season] concatenates results single tidy frame. Mirrors Stats.skater_stats_summary convenience helper nhl-api-py Python client.

title: NHL Skater Summary (Season Range) — nhl_skater_summary_range
code: nhl_skater_summary_range(start_season, end_season, game_type = 2, limit = 50)

title: NHL Skater Summary (Season Range) — nhl_skater_summary_range
text: start_season Integer four-digit end year first season (e.g. 2022 2021-22 season). end_season Integer four-digit end year final season (inclusive). Must >= start_season. game_type Integer game type: 2 = regular season (default), 3 = playoffs. limit Integer maximum number rows per season request. Defaults 50 match nhl_stats_skaters().

title: NHL Skater Summary (Season Range) — nhl_skater_summary_range

title: NHL Smart Links — nhl_smartlinks

title: NHL Smart Links — nhl_smartlinks
code: nhl_smartlinks(handle = NULL)

title: NHL Smart Links — nhl_smartlinks
text: handle Optional character handle. provided, lookup narrowed single smart link via handle query parameter.

title: NHL Standings — nhl_standings
text: Returns current historical NHL standings. Uses new NHL API (api-web.nhle.com).

title: NHL Standings — nhl_standings
code: nhl_standings(date = NULL)

title: NHL Standings — nhl_standings
text: date Character date "YYYY-MM-DD" format. NULL, returns current standings.

what: NHL Stats API — Content Module — nhl_stats_content_module
title: NHL Stats API — Content Module — nhl_stats_content_module
text: Returns NHL.com CMS content module Stats REST API (https://api.nhle.com/stats/rest/{lang}/content/module/{template_key}).

title: NHL Stats API — Content Module — nhl_stats_content_module
code: nhl_stats_content_module(template_key, lang = "en")

title: NHL Stats API — Content Module — nhl_stats_content_module
text: template_key Character (required). content module template key (NHL.com internal identifier). lang Character language code. Default "en".

title: NHL Stats API — Content Module — nhl_stats_content_module
text: fastRhockey_data tibble content module rows, NULL failure.

title: NHL Stats API — Content Module — nhl_stats_content_module
code: # \donttest{   try(nhl_stats_content_module(template_key = "example")) #> 2026-04-13 17:05:58.220846: No content module data for 'example' #> NULL # }

what: NHL Stats API — Country Lookup — nhl_stats_country
title: NHL Stats API — Country Lookup — nhl_stats_country
text: Returns country lookup list NHL Stats REST API (https://api.nhle.com/stats/rest/{lang}/country). Used player nationality mapping.

title: NHL Stats API — Country Lookup — nhl_stats_country
code: nhl_stats_country(lang = "en")

title: NHL Stats API — Country Lookup — nhl_stats_country
text: lang Character language code. Default "en".

title: NHL Stats API — Country Lookup — nhl_stats_country
text: fastRhockey_data tibble countries, NULL failure.

what: NHL Stats API — Draft Summaries — nhl_stats_draft
title: NHL Stats API — Draft Summaries — nhl_stats_draft
text: Queries NHL Stats REST API draft-year summaries (draft year number rounds). Optionally filter specific draft year via draft_year.

title: NHL Stats API — Draft Summaries — nhl_stats_draft
code: nhl_stats_draft(draft_year = NULL, limit = 100, start = 0, lang = "en")

title: NHL Stats API — Draft Summaries — nhl_stats_draft
text: draft_year Integer draft year (e.g., 2024). NULL, returns draft years (1963-present). limit Integer maximum number results. Default 100. start Integer start index pagination. Default 0. lang Character language code. Default "en".

title: NHL Stats API — Draft Summaries — nhl_stats_draft
text: Returns data frame columns: id, draft_year, rounds.

title: NHL Stats API — Draft Summaries — nhl_stats_draft

what: NHL Stats API — Franchise Listing — nhl_stats_franchise
title: NHL Stats API — Franchise Listing — nhl_stats_franchise
text: Returns franchise listing NHL Stats REST API (https://api.nhle.com/stats/rest/{lang}/franchise). Parallels nhl-api-py Teams.franchises() helper.

title: NHL Stats API — Franchise Listing — nhl_stats_franchise
code: nhl_stats_franchise(lang = "en", limit = 100, start = 0, cayenne_exp = NULL)

title: NHL Stats API — Franchise Listing — nhl_stats_franchise
text: lang Character language code. Default "en". limit Integer maximum number results. Default 100. start Integer pagination start index. Default 0. cayenne_exp Optional Cayenne filter expression string passed via cayenneExp query parameter (e.g., "id=1").

title: NHL Stats API — Franchise Listing — nhl_stats_franchise
text: fastRhockey_data tibble franchises, NULL failure.

what: NHL Stats API — Game Listing — nhl_stats_game_listing
title: NHL Stats API — Game Listing — nhl_stats_game_listing
text: Returns game listing NHL Stats REST API (https://api.nhle.com/stats/rest/{lang}/game). Supports server-side filtering via Cayenne expression.

title: NHL Stats API — Game Listing — nhl_stats_game_listing
code: nhl_stats_game_listing(lang = "en", limit = 100, start = 0, cayenne_exp = NULL)

title: NHL Stats API — Game Listing — nhl_stats_game_listing
text: lang Character language code. Default "en". limit Integer maximum number results. Default 100. start Integer pagination start index. Default 0. cayenne_exp Optional Cayenne filter expression string passed via cayenneExp query parameter (e.g., "season=20242025").

title: NHL Stats API — Game Listing — nhl_stats_game_listing
text: fastRhockey_data tibble games, NULL failure.

title: NHL Stats API — Game Listing — nhl_stats_game_listing
NHL Stats API — Glossary — nhl_stats_glossary
text: Returns glossary stat definitions NHL Stats REST API (https://api.nhle.com/stats/rest/{lang}/glossary). Useful look given abbreviation statistic (e.g., PIM) means.

title: NHL Stats API — Glossary — nhl_stats_glossary
code: nhl_stats_glossary(lang = "en")

title: NHL Stats API — Glossary — nhl_stats_glossary
text: lang Character language code. Default "en".

what: NHL Stats API — Miscellaneous Endpoints — nhl_stats_misc
title: NHL Stats API — Miscellaneous Endpoints — nhl_stats_misc
text: Generic dispatcher NHL Stats REST API (https://api.nhle.com/stats/rest/{lang}/{endpoint}). Pass valid endpoint name endpoint helper issue request return parsed data element data.frame. non-tabular endpoints, raw parsed list returned instead.

title: NHL Stats API — Miscellaneous Endpoints — nhl_stats_misc
code: nhl_stats_misc(endpoint = "glossary", game_id = NULL, lang = "en")

title: NHL Stats API — Miscellaneous Endpoints — nhl_stats_misc
text: endpoint Character endpoint path. Known valid values include: Tabular: "franchise", "glossary", "country", "config", "players", "team", "game", "componentSeason", "milestones/skaters", "milestones/goalies", "leaders/skaters/{attribute}", "leaders/goalies/{attribute}" Reference / lookup: "gameType", "shiftcharts" (use dedicated game_id argument one), "content/module/{templateKey}", "team/id/{id}" Health check: "ping" Note: dedicated wrappers exist season, draft, skater/{report}, goalie/{report}, team/{report} — see nhl_stats_seasons(), nhl_stats_draft(), nhl_stats_skaters(), nhl_stats_goalies(), nhl_stats_teams(). game_id Optional game ID (required endpoint = "shiftcharts"). lang Character language code. Default "en".

title: NHL Stats API — Miscellaneous Endpoints — nhl_stats_misc
text: Returns data frame (fastRhockey_data) tabular endpoints, raw parsed list non-tabular ones.

title: NHL Team Summary (Season Range) — nhl_team_summary_range
text: Aggregator helper calls nhl_stats_teams() report_type = "summary" every season [start_season, end_season] concatenates results single tidy frame. Mirrors Stats.team_summary convenience helper nhl-api-py Python client.

title: NHL Team Summary (Season Range) — nhl_team_summary_range
code: nhl_team_summary_range(start_season, end_season, game_type = 2, limit = 50)

title: NHL Team Summary (Season Range) — nhl_team_summary_range
text: start_season Integer four-digit end year first season (e.g. 2022 2021-22 season). end_season Integer four-digit end year final season (inclusive). Must >= start_season. game_type Integer game type: 2 = regular season (default), 3 = playoffs. limit Integer maximum number rows per season request. Defaults 50 match nhl_stats_teams().

title: NHL Team Summary (Season Range) — nhl_team_summary_range
text: fastRhockey_data / data.frame concatenated team summary stats added season column marking season row came . Returns NULL outer failure.

title: NHL Teams Stats — nhl_teams_stats
text: Returns NHL team player-level stats (skaters goalies) given team abbreviation season. Uses new NHL API club-stats endpoint (api-web.nhle.com). Breaking change: old team_id (integer) parameter replaced team_abbr (3-letter string). season parameter now accepts 4-digit year (e.g., 2024 2024-25 season).

title: NHL Teams Stats — nhl_teams_stats
code: nhl_teams_stats(team_abbr, season = NULL, game_type = 2)

title: NHL Teams Stats — nhl_teams_stats
text: team_abbr Three-letter team abbreviation (e.g., "TBL", "TOR", "SEA") season Integer 4-digit year (e.g., 2024 2024-25 season). NULL, returns current season stats. game_type Integer game type: 2 = regular season (default), 3 = playoffs

title: NHL Teams Stats — nhl_teams_stats
text: Returns data frame per-player stats team, including skaters goalies identified player_type column.

title: NHL WSC Play-by-Play — nhl_wsc_pbp
text: Returns narrative-format (Web Services Content) play--play feed given NHL game ID NHL web service. endpoint distinct nhl_game_pbp(), hits gamecenter/{id}/play--play endpoint returns tabular play-event feed. nhl_wsc_pbp() hits wsc/play--play/{gameId} returns narrative-format payload used NHL.com's story / recap pages (closer shape nhl_game_story()).

title: Update or create a fastRhockey NHL play-by-play database — update_nhl_db
text: dbdir Directory database shall located dbname File name existing desired SQLite database within dbdir tblname name play play data table within database force_rebuild Hybrid parameter (logical numeric) rebuild parts complete play play data table within database (please see details information) db_connection DBIConnection object, returned DBI::dbConnect() (please see details information)

title: Update or create a fastRhockey NHL play-by-play database — update_nhl_db
text: Logical TRUE/FALSE

title: Update or create a fastRhockey NHL play-by-play database — update_nhl_db
text: function creates updates data table name tblname within SQLite database (drivers via db_connection) located dbdir named dbname. data table combines play play data every available game back 2010 season adds recent completed games soon available fastRhockey. argument force_rebuild hybrid type. can rebuild play play data table either whole fastRhockey era (force_rebuild = TRUE) just specified seasons (e.g. force_rebuild = c(2019, 2020)). Please note following behavior: force_rebuild = TRUE: data table name tblname removed completely rebuilt scratch. helpful new columns added -Season. force_rebuild = c(2019, 2020): data table name tblname preserved rows 2019 2020 seasons deleted re-added. intended used ongoing seasons ESPN's data provider can make changes underlying data week. parameter db_connection intended advanced users want use DBI drivers, MariaDB, Postgres odbc. Please note arguments dbdir dbname dropped case db_connection provided argument tblname still used write data table database.

what: PWHL parity: 3 new NHL loaders + datasets
title: fastRhockey 1.0.0 (continued development)
text: Three new season-level NHL loaders bring NHL coverage line PWHL. backed new release tag sportsdataverse/sportsdataverse-data powered new extractors fastRhockey-nhl-raw/R/scrape_nhl_raw.R fastRhockey-nhl-data/R/nhl_data_creation.R.

title: fastRhockey 1.0.0 (continued development)
text: nhl_schedule() gained include_data_flags = FALSE parameter. TRUE, live schedule left-joined cached nhl_games_in_data_repo index data repo gains 16 logical columns (PBP, team_box, player_box, skater_box, goalie_box, game_info, game_rosters, scoring, penalties, scratches, linescore, three_stars, shifts, officials, shots_by_period, shootout) telling pre-compiled datasets cover game. schedule files fastRhockey-nhl-raw/nhl/schedules/ master schedule data repo carry flags.

title: fastRhockey 1.0.0 (continued development)
text: scrape_nhl_raw.R now writes four additional top-level keys nhl/json/raw/{game_id}.json nhl/json/final/{game_id}.json: officials — list {role, name} (referees first, linesmen second) shots_by_period — long-form per-team-per-period shot totals shootout — per-attempt summary (NULL game didn’t reach ) plays_by_period — OLD-format index play_indices per period on_ice / on_ice_plus / penalty_box (final-state reconstruction, derived processed PBP) — populated final/ variant since need fastRhockey-enriched -ice columns

what: Refactor: NHL loaders consolidated into R/nhl_loaders.R
title: fastRhockey 1.0.0 (continued development)
text: NHL season-level loaders shared .nhl_release_loader() helper now live R/nhl_loaders.R, mirroring PWHL convention (R/pwhl_loaders.R). empty R/nhl_pbp.R file removed since never contained actual nhl_pbp() scraper — loaders. Public signatures return types unchanged.

title: fastRhockey 1.0.0 (continued development)
text: Wraps NHL Edge advanced-metrics endpoints https://api-web.nhle.com/v1/edge/.... Every wrapper accepts optional season argument (4-digit end year, e.g., 2025) — omitted, /now form used fetch current season. wrappers share internal .nhl_edge_api() helper R/helpers_nhl_edge.R.

what: New NHL Records API integration (25 functions)
title: fastRhockey 1.0.0 (continued development)
text: First-time integration https://records.nhl.com/site/api/. wrappers share internal .nhl_records_api() helper R/helpers_nhl_records.R supports cayenneExp filters, limit/start pagination, records-API response shape ({data, total}). Franchise: nhl_records_franchise(), nhl_records_franchise_detail(), nhl_records_franchise_totals(), nhl_records_franchise_team_totals(), nhl_records_franchise_season_results(), nhl_records_franchise_playoff_appearances() Player: nhl_records_player(), nhl_records_player_byteam(), nhl_records_player_stats(), nhl_records_skater_real_time_stats_season(), nhl_records_skater_real_time_stats_career() Goalie: nhl_records_goalie_career_stats(), nhl_records_goalie_season_stats(), nhl_records_goalie_shutout_streak() Draft: nhl_records_draft(), nhl_records_draft_lottery_odds(), nhl_records_draft_lottery_picks(), nhl_records_draft_prospect() Awards / HOF: nhl_records_trophy(), nhl_records_award_details(), nhl_records_hof_players() League: nhl_records_officials(), nhl_records_attendance(), nhl_records_venue(), nhl_records_combine()

what: New NHL Stats REST wrappers (13 functions)
title: fastRhockey 1.0.0 (continued development)
text: Promotes endpoints previously reachable via nhl_stats_misc()’s generic dispatcher discoverable, dedicated wrappers. Documentation nhl_stats_misc() also updated enumerate every valid endpoint value. nhl_stats_franchise(), nhl_stats_players(), nhl_stats_glossary(), nhl_stats_country(), nhl_stats_config(), nhl_stats_ping() nhl_stats_skater_leaders(), nhl_stats_goalie_leaders(), nhl_stats_skater_milestones(), nhl_stats_goalie_milestones() nhl_stats_team_listing(), nhl_stats_game_listing(), nhl_stats_content_module()

what: New NHL api-web miscellaneous endpoints (6 functions + 3 in-place updates)
title: fastRhockey 1.0.0 (continued development)
text: nhl_wsc_pbp(game_id) – narrative-format play--play wsc/play--play/{gameId} (distinct nhl_game_pbp() uses gamecenter/{id}/play--play). nhl_draft_tracker() – live draft tracker (draft-tracker/picks/now), distinct nhl_draft() hits static draft/picks/now endpoint. nhl_ppt_replay(game_id, event_number) – event-level replay metadata. nhl_ppt_replay_goal(game_id, event_number) – goal-specific replay metadata. nhl_postal_lookup(postal_code) – broadcast region lookup postal code. nhl_smartlinks(handle = NULL) – NHL.com smart-link router. nhl_scoreboard(date = NULL) – now accepts optional date argument callers can fetch historical scoreboards (hardcoded /now). nhl_meta(game_id = NULL, year = NULL, series_letter = NULL) – added third branch meta/playoff-series/{year}/{seriesLetter}. nhl_draft_year(year, round = NULL) – round NULL "", function now hits /draft/picks/{year}/shortcut single request instead looping per round.

what: New helper aggregators inspired by nhl-api-py (6 functions)
title: fastRhockey 1.0.0 (continued development)
text: Convenience wrappers orchestrate multiple endpoint calls one tidy data frame: nhl_game_ids_by_season(season, game_types, team_abbr, sleep_rate) – iterates every team’s season schedule returns deduplicated set game IDs. nhl_all_players_by_season(season, sleep_rate) – iterates every team’s roster flattens forwards/defensemen/goalies. nhl_player_career_stats(player_id) – combines player/{id}/landing season--season seasonTotals payload career frame. nhl_team_summary_range(start_season, end_season) – multi-season team summary loop nhl_stats_teams(report_type = "summary"). nhl_skater_summary_range(start_season, end_season) – skater summary. nhl_goalie_summary_range(start_season, end_season) – goalie summary.

title: fastRhockey 1.0.0 (continued development)
text: data-raw/nhl_missing_endpoint_function_mapping.md – table every documented NHL API endpoint proposed/implemented fastRhockey wrapper, sourced dfleis/nhl-api-docs, RentoSaijo/nhlscraper, coreyjs/nhl-api-py. data-raw/nhl_api_web_openapi.{json,yaml} – OpenAPI 3.0.3 spec api-web.nhle.com/v1/ (132 endpoints). data-raw/nhl_stats_rest_openapi.{json,yaml} – OpenAPI 3.0.3 spec api.nhle.com/stats/rest/{lang}/ (21 endpoints). data-raw/nhl_records_openapi.{json,yaml} – OpenAPI 3.0.3 spec records.nhl.com/site/api/ (442 endpoints). data-raw/_gen_openapi.py – regenerator script.

title: fastRhockey 1.0.0 (continued development)
text: nhl_stats_players() now detects data: [] empty-list responses (API returns cayenne_exp filter supplied) returns NULL friendly hint instead crashing clean_names(). nhl_stats_skater_leaders() nhl_stats_goalie_leaders() longer pass start/limit query parameters, leaders/{skaters,goalies}/{attribute} endpoint rejects 500 Cayenne SQL error. Roxygen now documents valid attribute values: skaters accept assists/goals/points; goalies accept savePctg/gaa/shutouts. nhl_records_franchise(franchise_id = ...) now translates franchise_id argument cayenneExp=id={id} filter. records API support path-suffix franchise/{id} form (returns 404). nhl_records_award_details() – replaced broken franchise_id argument season_id (award-details endpoint accepts seasonId filtering rejects franchiseId). nhl_stats_content_module() now guards unnamed CMS responses previously crashed clean_names().

title: fastRhockey 1.0.0 (continued development)
text: Ten new season-level loaders pull pre-compiled NHL datasets new release tags sportsdataverse/sportsdataverse-data. accept seasons vector (Min: 2011) return fastRhockey_data data frame, mirroring existing load_nhl_*() API: six pre-existing loaders (load_nhl_pbp(), load_nhl_pbp_lite(), load_nhl_player_box(), load_nhl_team_box(), load_nhl_schedule(), load_nhl_rosters()) refactored share single internal worker (.nhl_release_loader()) without changing public signatures return types.

title: fastRhockey 1.0.0 (continued development)
text: Eleven new season-level loaders pull pre-compiled PWHL datasets new release tags sportsdataverse/sportsdataverse-data. accept seasons vector (Min: 2024) return fastRhockey_data data frame, mirroring existing load_pwhl_*() API: four pre-existing loaders (load_pwhl_pbp(), load_pwhl_player_box(), load_pwhl_schedule(), load_pwhl_rosters()) refactored share single internal worker (.pwhl_release_loader()) without changing public signatures return types.

title: fastRhockey 1.0.0
text: Version 1.0.0 signals API stability NHL PWHL endpoints. PHF functions formally deprecated via lifecycle. Premier Hockey Federation ceased operations; use PWHL functions instead. Functions removed future release. Consolidated new NHL API functions existing NHL function names rather creating _v2 variants, since original API endpoints deprecated NHL. means nhl_game_feed(), nhl_game_boxscore(), nhl_schedule(), nhl_teams(), nhl_teams_roster(), nhl_player_info() now use new api-web.nhle.com endpoints directly.

title: fastRhockey 1.0.0
text: pwhl_pbp() – PWHL play--play data. pwhl_player_box() – PWHL player box scores (skaters goalies). pwhl_game_info() – PWHL game information metadata. pwhl_game_summary() – Detailed game summary (scoring, penalties, shots period, three stars). pwhl_standings() – PWHL league standings. pwhl_stats() – PWHL stat leaders (skaters goalies). pwhl_leaders() – League leaders (top scorers top goalies). pwhl_season_id() – PWHL season ID lookup (now API-driven fallback). pwhl_player_info() – Player biographical profile information. pwhl_player_game_log() – Per-game statistics player season. pwhl_player_stats() – Career season--season statistics player. pwhl_player_search() – Search players name. pwhl_transactions() – Player transactions season. pwhl_streaks() – Player streak data season. pwhl_playoff_bracket() – Playoff bracket / series data. pwhl_scorebar() – Recent upcoming game scores. most_recent_pwhl_season() – Utility get current PWHL season year. load_pwhl_pbp() – Load pre-scraped PWHL play--play data. load_pwhl_player_box() – Load pre-scraped PWHL player box scores. load_pwhl_schedule() – Load pre-scraped PWHL schedules. load_pwhl_rosters() – Load pre-scraped PWHL team rosters. update_pwhl_db() – Update create PWHL play--play database.

title: fastRhockey 1.0.0
text: nhl_where_to_watch() – streaming/broadcast availability. nhl_partner_game_odds() – partner game odds country. NHL API migration api-web.nhle.com: nhl_game_feed(), nhl_game_boxscore(), nhl_schedule(), nhl_teams(), nhl_teams_roster(), nhl_player_info(), nhl_player_game_log(), nhl_stats_goalies(), nhl_stats_skaters(), nhl_stats_teams(), nhl_stats_misc(), nhl_gamecenter_landing(), nhl_gamecenter_right_rail(), nhl_scoreboard(), nhl_scores(), nhl_seasons(), nhl_tv_schedule(), nhl_game_story(), nhl_game_content(), nhl_game_shifts(), nhl_playoff_bracket(), nhl_playoff_schedule(), nhl_playoff_carousel(). xG model integration via helper_nhl_calculate_xg().

title: fastRhockey 1.0.0
text: Fixed nhl_draft_year() – NHL API removed /v1/draft/picks/{year} endpoint. function now iterates rounds 1-7 using /v1/draft/picks/{year}/{round}. Fixed pwhl_stats() – resolved “object ‘players’ found” error API calls failed. Fixed team ID resolution skater stats. Fixed pwhl_schedule() – season column inadvertently dropped output. now included. Fixed refresh_xg_models() – resolved “change value locked binding” error storing xG models package environment (.xg_env) instead top-level bindings. Fixed NAMESPACE: removed import(tidyverse) violated CRAN policy. Individual packages (dplyr, tidyr, etc.) already imported.

title: fastRhockey 1.0.0
text: PHF functions: phf_schedule(), phf_standings(), phf_pbp(), phf_player_box(), phf_team_box(), phf_team_roster(), phf_team_stats(), phf_player_stats(), phf_leaders(), phf_league_info(), phf_game_all(), phf_game_raw(), phf_game_details(), phf_game_summary(). PHF loaders: load_phf_pbp(), load_phf_team_box(), load_phf_player_box(), load_phf_schedule(), load_phf_rosters(), update_phf_db(). Utility: most_recent_phf_season().

title: fastRhockey 1.0.0
text: pwhl_season_id() now retrieves season data dynamically HockeyTech API instead using hardcoded lookup table. Falls back hardcoded data API unavailable. Added internal helpers .pwhl_api(), .pwhl_modulekit_url(), .pwhl_gc_url(), .pwhl_resolve_season_id() reduce JSONP parsing boilerplate across PWHL functions. Added lifecycle package formal deprecation management. Updated testthat dependency >= 3.0.0. Complete test coverage 95 exported functions (482 tests). Added environment-controlled test toggles (RUN_NHL_TESTS, RUN_PHF_TESTS, RUN_PWHL_TESTS) via tests/testthat/helper-skip.R. CI workflow environment variables now match helper-skip.R names. Updated test expectations match current API responses. nhl_where_to_watch() returns NULL gracefully NHL /v1/--watch endpoint unavailable. Updated _pkgdown.yml reorganized reference sections deprecated function categories.

title: fastRhockey 1.0.0
text: Updated CONTRIBUTING.md naming conventions, testing environment variables, conventional commits guide, deprecation process. Updated PR issue templates. Updated CLAUDE.md .github/copilot-instructions.md reflect v1.0.0 changes new PWHL endpoints. Added data-raw/pr_devel.md development scratchpad. Added cran_comments.md CRAN submission. Updated _pkgdown.yml reorganized PWHL reference sections. Added R CMD check CI workflow pkgdown deployment workflow.
